_meta.title: drone_provenance_ppk
_meta.description: Frozen reference for drone_score (PPK workflow), revised post BIN-consolidation. A single ArduPilot .BIN replaces four previously-separate sources (Mission Plan, Flight Telemetry Log, Flight Data Record, MRK file). New sources: SRC_UI_01 (2-field JSON metadata form) and SRC_API_01 (Open-Meteo weather API). All FDR fields are now live (is_aspirational=FALSE everywhere). mission_pattern removed. RTK-only and No-Correction-only fields/indicators/blocks excluded.

critical_gates.rover_continuity_hard_rule: if critical_gap_present == True -> rover_continuity_score = 0 (raises RINEX_CRITICAL_GAP). Heavily penalises BB_ROVER_GNSS but does not zero drone_score by itself.

_meta.cal_conf_note: CAL_CONF is a parallel deliverable with weight_in_drone_score_ppk = 0.00. It travels with the camera calibration file to the Processing Universe. Computed inside the drone subsystem from the 7 calibration identity/provenance fields (L1F_CAL_001–007). The 8 distortion fields (L1F_CAL_008–015) are captured here but consumed by the Processing Universe (ODM), not by drone_score.
_meta.bin_consolidation_note: A single ArduPilot .BIN flight log replaces four previously-separate sources: SRC_FC_01 (Mission Plan), SRC_FC_02 (Flight Telemetry Log), SRC_FC_03 (Flight Data Record) and SRC_GNSS_02 (MRK image timestamp file). Within the BIN, CMD messages carry the mission, GPS/POS/CTUN/BAT/MISE/MODE/ARM carry telemetry, IMU/VIBE/ATT/CAM carry FDR data, and CAM messages alone fully replace the MRK file (one record per shutter trigger with GPS time + week + lat/lng/alt + roll/pitch/yaw). Result: only 3 real files are needed from the pilot — images, RINEX, and the .BIN — plus a 2-field JSON metadata form and one Open-Meteo API call.
_meta.user_input_form_note: SRC_UI_01 is a 2-field JSON pre-flight metadata form: planned_overlap_fwd_pct and planned_overlap_lat_pct. These are the only values that are pure operator intent and cannot be recovered from the BIN. planned_altitude_m, planned_speed_ms, planned_waypoint_count and takeoff coordinates all come from BIN CMD waypoints. planned_area_m2 and planned_gsd_cm are now derived. mission_pattern has been removed entirely (it fed no indicator).
_meta.external_api_note: SRC_API_01 is the Open-Meteo historical weather API, queried automatically using takeoff lat/lng and flight_start_utc derived from the BIN. Supplies mean_wind_speed_ms for wind_condition_score. A BIN-internal fallback proxy (ATT roll/pitch stdev) is available if the API call fails.
changelog_v1_1_1: FIX 1: derived_fields column renamed input_source_field_ids -> input_field_ids (column accepts both L1F_ source-field refs and L2D_ derived-field refs; new name is accurate).
FIX 2: flags sheet gained raised_at_stage column with values threshold_band | internal_gate | global_gate | pre_score_ingestion. Tells an agent where each flag fires.
FIX 3: _meta.audit_counts corrected (script_hints 23 -> 24 to include drone_score rollup hint; drone_score_blocks counted from drone_score.weights list).
_meta.previous: 1.1.1 (pre-Q-DRONE locks; duplicate calibration indicators; only 1 chain-level hard gate; no severity vocabulary; no null handling)

q_locks_applied.Q-DRONE-1: Deduplicate calibration indicators — removed L3I_IMG_006/007 from BB_IMG_CAPTURE; retained L3I_CAL_001/002 in BB_CAL_CONF. BB_IMG_CAPTURE renormalized.
q_locks_applied.Q-DRONE-2: 3 chain-level hard gates promoted: image_validity (existing), rover_coverage (new), mission_coverage (new).
q_locks_applied.Q-DRONE-3: BB_CAL_CONF retained as 4th block, weight 0.10. Calibration is operationally distinct from image quality.
q_locks_applied.Q-DRONE-4: Wind API failure → indicator-level null (new pattern). L3I_FC_005 returns null when Open-Meteo API unavailable, NOT fabricated score.
q_locks_applied.Q-DRONE-5: SELF_CALIBRATED_LENS stays MEDIUM (material). Flagging at review level lets operator decide based on accuracy requirements.

_meta.design_summary: Four-block evidence-based drone confidence chain. PPK workflow. Largest chain to date (4 blocks, 21 indicators after Q-DRONE-1 dedup). Confidence-only output: score (0-100) + tier + recommendation. THREE chain-level hard gates (Q-DRONE-2): image validity, rover coverage, mission coverage. Q-DRONE-4 introduces indicator-level null pattern (wind API failure → null, not fabricated score). Severity vocabulary at band level: none / minor / material / critical. Recommendation vocabulary: good_to_go / review_recommended / resurvey_recommended.

new_pattern_concepts.indicator_level_null: Q-DRONE-4: First chain to introduce indicator-level null (distinct from GCP's chain-level null). When a specific indicator's input is unavailable, that indicator returns null and block aggregation renormalizes across measured indicators. Composite scoring must respect indicator-null vs zero.
new_pattern_concepts.multiple_chain_level_hard_gates: Q-DRONE-2: First chain with 3 chain-level hard gates. Pattern: multiple critical-path indicators can each independently kill the chain. global_gate_condition string lists all gates with OR.

readme.TITLE: drone_provenance_ppk — Frozen reference, PPK workflow (revised post BIN-consolidation)
readme.PURPOSE: Frozen single source of truth for drone_score (PPK workflow). Used both as human reference and as machine-readable contract for the ingestion pipeline. Reflects the decision to consolidate Flight Controller sources around a single ArduPilot .BIN file plus a tiny JSON user-input form and one external weather API.
readme.SCOPE: PPK workflow ONLY. RTK-only and No-Correction-only fields/indicators/blocks are excluded.
readme.SOURCE: Originally extracted from drone_provenance_v3_1.html. This revision applies the consolidation decisions captured in the "Drone source files and systems" working session (May 2026).
readme.HOW TO USE: Each row is one atomic fact. Join sheets via *_id columns: source_fields.file_id → source_files.file_id; derived_fields.input_source_field_ids → source_fields.field_id (semicolon-separated); indicators.building_block_id → building_blocks.block_id; thresholds.indicator_id → indicators.indicator_id; block_composition.block_id+indicator_id → many-to-many between blocks and indicators.
readme.ID PREFIXES: SRC_ = source file. L1F_ = level-1 source field. L2D_ = level-2 derived field. L3I_ = level-3 indicator. BB_ = building block. TH_ = threshold band. FLG_ = flag.
readme.CRITICAL GATES: Two gates can short-circuit drone_score to 0: (a) BB_IMG_CAPTURE internal gate: image_validity_score < 30 → image_capture_score = 0 → drone_score = 0 (FLG_001, FLG_016). (b) L3I_GNSS_004 hard rule: critical_gap_present == True → rover_continuity_score = 0 (does not zero drone_score by itself, but heavily penalises BB_ROVER_GNSS).
readme.CAL_CONF: BB_CAL_CONF is a PARALLEL deliverable. weight_in_drone_score_ppk = 0.00. It travels with the camera calibration file to the Processing Universe and informs ODM how much to trust its own lens parameters. CAL_CONF is computed inside the drone subsystem from the 7 calibration identity/provenance fields (L1F_CAL_001–007). The 8 distortion fields (L1F_CAL_008–015) are also captured here but are consumed by the Processing Universe (ODM), not by drone_score itself.
readme.THRESHOLD SEMANTICS: Thresholds in sheet 05 are written as machine-parseable expressions (condition_expression). band_order=1 is the highest score band; bands are evaluated in order and the first true band wins.
readme.BIN CONSOLIDATION (KEY CHANGE): A single ArduPilot .BIN flight log replaces four previously-separate sources: SRC_FC_01 (Mission Plan), SRC_FC_02 (Flight Telemetry Log), SRC_FC_03 (Flight Data Record) and SRC_GNSS_02 (MRK image timestamp file). Within the BIN, CMD messages carry the mission, GPS/POS/CTUN/BAT/MISE/MODE/ARM carry telemetry, IMU/VIBE/ATT/CAM carry FDR data, and CAM messages alone fully replace the MRK file (one record per shutter trigger with GPS time + week + lat/lng/alt + roll/pitch/yaw). Result: only 3 real files are needed from the pilot — images, RINEX, and the .BIN — plus a 2-field JSON metadata form and one Open-Meteo API call.
readme.ASPIRATIONAL FIELDS: is_aspirational is now FALSE for ALL fields. The previously-aspirational SRC_FC_03 (Flight Data Record) is fully delivered by the ArduPilot .BIN with no DatCon tooling required. vibration_score and gimbal_score are no longer disabled.
readme.USER-INPUT FORM: SRC_UI_01 is a 2-field JSON pre-flight metadata form: planned_overlap_fwd_pct and planned_overlap_lat_pct. These are the only values that are pure operator intent and cannot be recovered from the BIN. planned_altitude_m, planned_speed_ms, planned_waypoint_count and takeoff coordinates all come from BIN CMD waypoints. planned_area_m2 and planned_gsd_cm are now derived. mission_pattern has been removed entirely (it fed no indicator).
readme.EXTERNAL API: SRC_API_01 is the Open-Meteo historical weather API, queried automatically using takeoff lat/lng and flight_start_utc derived from the BIN. Supplies mean_wind_speed_ms for wind_condition_score. A BIN-internal fallback proxy (ATT roll/pitch stdev) is available if the API call fails.
readme.COMPLETENESS: RTK-only and No-Correction-only items remain excluded. All FDR items active. mission_pattern removed. All reclassified fields moved to 03_DerivedFields or to SRC_UI_01 / SRC_API_01 as appropriate.
readme.01_SourceFiles: file_id | file_name | subsystem | file_extensions | physical_source | how_to_obtain | is_aspirational | notes. Reality column added.
readme.02_SourceFields: field_id | field_name | file_id (FK to 01) | subsystem | category | meaning | data_type | units | acquisition_mode | is_aspirational | notes.
readme.03_DerivedFields: derived_id | derived_name | subsystem | formula_expression (machine) | formula_notes | input_field_ids (semicolon list; may contain L1F_ source-field refs and/or L2D_ derived-field refs) | meaning | units
readme.04_Indicators: indicator_id | indicator_name | display_name | building_block_id (FK to 06) | weight_in_block | meaning | input_derived_field_ids | input_source_field_ids | source_chain_text | has_internal_gate | gate_condition | gate_action.
readme.05_Thresholds: threshold_id | indicator_id (FK to 04) | band_order (1=highest score) | condition_expression | condition_text | score_value | flag_raised.
readme.06_BuildingBlocks: block_id | block_name | display_name | purpose | weight_in_drone_score_ppk | formula_expression | has_internal_gate | gate_condition | gate_action | disabled_in_workflows | weight_redistribution_rule.
readme.07_BlockComposition: block_id (FK to 06) | indicator_id (FK to 04) | weight. Many-to-many table mirroring sheet 04 weights.
readme.08_DroneScore: block_id (FK to 06) | weight_in_ppk | notes. Plus a metadata block above the table.
readme.09_Flags: flag_id | flag_name | raised_by_id (FK to indicator/block/score/source_field/derived) | raised_by_type | raised_at_stage (threshold_band | internal_gate | global_gate | pre_score_ingestion) | condition | severity | meaning
readme.10_ScriptHints: indicator_id (FK to 04) | indicator_name | pseudocode (Python-style template implementing the threshold ladder).

source_files.how_to_obtain: Remove the SD card from the drone camera after the flight and copy all image files. EXIF metadata (GPS position, camera settings, timestamps) is embedded in each file.
source_files.notes: PPK workflow uses approximate (2–5m) EXIF GPS positions; precise positions are recomputed in Stage 2 from the RINEX file using PPK. EXIF still feeds image-to-RINEX-epoch matching when MRK-equivalent CAM messages are unavailable.

source_files.physical_source: CB camera library, ODM database, or self-calibration (no physical file from pilot)
source_files.how_to_obtain: Three sources in priority order: (1) CB camera library — curated parameters matched by EXIF Make+Model (highest confidence, CB_LIBRARY); (2) ODM built-in database — looked up automatically by ODM if not in CB library (ODM_DATABASE); (3) self-calibration — ODM estimates parameters from the images themselves during reconstruction (SELF_CALIBRATED). The pipeline records which path was taken via calibration_source. No pilot action required.
source_files.notes: Identity + geometry fields (L1F_CAL_001–007) feed image_capture_score and CAL_CONF inside the drone subsystem. Distortion fields (L1F_CAL_008–015) are captured here but consumed by the Processing Universe (ODM lens correction); they do not feed drone_score directly. CAL_CONF travels with the calibration file to the Processing Universe.

source_files.physical_source: Downloaded from drone GNSS receiver internal storage
source_files.how_to_obtain: The GNSS receiver records raw satellite observations (.obs) and broadcast ephemerides (.nav) continuously during the flight. Downloaded from the receiver after landing — typically bundled as one logical source. PRIMARY source for PPK workflow.
source_files.notes: Raw observations are the raw material for PPK post-processing. .obs and .nav are always downloaded together and treated as one source. pre_buffer_sec and post_buffer_sec are technically computed by joining with flight timing derived from the BIN, but are surfaced as source-level fields for convenience.

source_files.physical_source: Internal flight controller storage (Pixhawk / Cube / similar ArduPilot autopilot)
source_files.how_to_obtain: Written by the flight controller to internal memory during the flight. Downloaded via Mission Planner, QGroundControl, or pymavlink directly from the FC over USB after landing. A single binary log file (typically 20–50 MB for a survey flight) that contains all of: mission waypoints (CMD), per-image shutter records (CAM), positions and altitudes (GPS/POS/CTUN), battery state (BAT), mission execution events (MISE), flight mode transitions (MODE), arm/disarm events (ARM/EV), IMU samples (IMU), vibration (VIBE), and attitude (ATT).
source_files.notes: CONSOLIDATED SOURCE — replaces the four previously-separate sources: SRC_FC_01 Mission Plan (CMD messages), SRC_FC_02 Flight Telemetry Log (GPS/POS/CTUN/BAT/MISE/MODE), SRC_FC_03 Flight Data Record (IMU/VIBE/ATT/CAM gimbal), and SRC_GNSS_02 MRK file (CAM messages, one per shutter trigger with GPS week + time-of-week + lat/lng/alt + roll/pitch/yaw). All previously-aspirational FDR fields are now live.

source_files.physical_source: Pre-flight or post-flight user input form (web/desktop UI)
source_files.how_to_obtain: A small JSON form with exactly 2 fields the operator enters once per mission: planned_overlap_fwd_pct and planned_overlap_lat_pct. These are pure operator intent and are not encoded in the .BIN waypoint geometry without circular dependencies on camera calibration. All other previously user-set values (planned_altitude_m, planned_speed_ms, planned_waypoint_count, takeoff coordinates) are now sourced directly from the BIN CMD messages.
source_files.notes: New source introduced as part of the BIN-consolidation decision. Replaces the need for a separate Mission Plan file export from the GCS app. mission_pattern was originally a candidate for this form but has been removed — it fed no indicator and can be inferred from waypoint geometry if ever needed.

source_files.physical_source: api.open-meteo.com (free, no API key required, historical archive)
source_files.how_to_obtain: Pipeline issues an automated HTTPS GET using takeoff latitude/longitude (from BIN NAV_TAKEOFF CMD) and flight_start_utc (derived from BIN GPS week + time-of-week at the ARM event). Returns hourly wind speed at 10m height for the closest grid cell. Pipeline averages over the flight window to produce mean_wind_speed_ms.
source_files.notes: Sole external dependency. Fallback: if the API call fails or no connectivity, an internal proxy based on ATT roll/pitch standard deviation from the BIN is used to band wind_condition_score conservatively (calm / moderate / unknown).

source_fields.meaning: Total number of image files found on the SD card for this flight. Counted automatically by the ingestion script.
Images that open and parse without errors or file corruption. Corrupted files are excluded from downstream processing.
Images with GPS coordinates present in EXIF. Even approximate coordinates from the onboard GNSS chip count.
File format of the images: JPG (compressed, smaller) or DNG/RAW (full sensor data, larger).
Latitude at time of capture from onboard GNSS chip. Approximate (2–5m) in PPK workflow.
source_fields.notes: PPK workflow: approximate position, corrected in Stage 2.

source_fields.meaning: Longitude at time of capture from onboard GNSS chip.
Altitude above sea level at capture from onboard GNSS chip.
UTC time of capture. Written by camera firmware from GNSS time signal. Used to sync each image with the BIN CAM record / RINEX epoch.
source_fields.notes: Used for image-to-RINEX-epoch matching when CAM records or MRK are absent.

source_fields.meaning: Camera manufacturer name. Written by camera firmware. Used to match against the calibration file Make.
Camera model name. Used alongside Make to identify the correct lens calibration entry in the CB library or ODM database.
Pixel width of the image sensor output. Determines GSD together with altitude and focal length.
Pixel height of the image sensor output.
Lens focal length in mm as reported by camera firmware. Used in GSD calculation. Less precise than the calibration file value.
Focus distance. Indicates whether the camera was correctly focused at survey altitude (typically should be infinity).
Sensor sensitivity setting. High ISO can introduce noise affecting image quality.
Shutter speed in seconds. Long exposures at high drone speed cause motion blur, especially at low light.
Aperture f-stop. Affects depth of field and image sharpness across the survey area.
Camera manufacturer in the calibration file. Must match EXIF.Make for the calibration to be valid for this hardware.
Camera model in the calibration file. Must match EXIF.Model.
Calibrated focal length in millimetres. More precise than the EXIF value which is rounded by camera firmware. Feeds GSD and overlap computations.
Physical sensor width. Combined with focal length and altitude to compute true GSD and image footprint.
Physical sensor height.
Where the calibration came from: CB_LIBRARY, ODM_DATABASE, or SELF_CALIBRATED. Determines confidence level.
source_fields.notes: Drives calibration_source_score in both image_capture_score and CAL_CONF.

source_fields.meaning: Date the camera calibration was last performed or verified. Used to compute calibration_age_months.

Horizontal offset of the optical centre from the image centre (cx). Corrects for manufacturing imperfection where the optical centre is not perfectly centred on the sensor. Consumed by ODM, not by drone_score.
source_fields.notes: HANDOFF TO PROCESSING — used by ODM lens correction; no drone_score dependency.

source_fields.meaning: Vertical offset of the optical centre (cy). Consumed by ODM.
Radial distortion coefficient k1. Corrects barrel or pincushion distortion. Consumed by ODM during reconstruction.
source_fields.notes: HANDOFF TO PROCESSING. Part of k1,k2,k3 radial distortion set.

source_fields.meaning: Radial distortion coefficient k2.
Radial distortion coefficient k3.
Tangential distortion coefficient p1. Corrects asymmetric distortion from lens not being perfectly parallel to sensor.
Tangential distortion coefficient p2.
Boolean: the calibration file is in a format that ODM can directly consume without conversion.
UTC timestamp when the receiver began recording. Should start before takeoff to ensure full coverage.
UTC timestamp when recording stopped. Should continue after landing.
Total recording duration in seconds. Compared against flight_duration_sec to compute coverage ratio.
Seconds of recording before takeoff (obs_start_utc minus flight_start_utc). PPK ambiguity resolution benefits from generous pre-flight buffer.
source_fields.notes: Computed by joining Rover RINEX with BIN ARM event — surfaced as source-level for convenience.

source_fields.meaning: Seconds of recording after landing (obs_end_utc minus flight_end_utc). Post-flight buffer aids PPK fixed-solution convergence.
Total observation epochs recorded. At 1Hz sampling, equals obs_duration_sec.
Sampling rate of the receiver. Higher rate gives better PPK temporal resolution.
Mean carrier-to-noise ratio across all satellites and epochs in dBHz. Values above 35 dBHz indicate clean signal.
Minimum C/N0 recorded. Persistent low values indicate signal obstruction or multipath.
Mean number of satellites tracked per epoch. More satellites improves PPK solution geometry.
Minimum satellites in any epoch. Below 4 means no valid position can be computed for that epoch.
Number of loss-of-lock events (LLI flags in RINEX). Cycle slips corrupt the carrier phase solution.
Fraction of epochs with both L1 and L2 observations. Dual frequency enables ionospheric correction.
Fraction of epochs with L1, L2, and L5 observations. Triple frequency further improves precision.
Number of GNSS constellations tracked: GPS, GLONASS, Galileo, BeiDou. More constellations improves geometry and redundancy.
Number of recording gaps longer than 5 seconds. Gaps interrupt the carrier phase solution and force re-acquisition.
Boolean: any single gap exceeding 60 seconds. A gap this long during a short survey effectively breaks PPK.
source_fields.notes: Drives critical_gap_present derived and rover_continuity_score hard rule.

source_fields.meaning: Receiver hardware model from RINEX header. Used to apply receiver-specific corrections during processing.
Antenna model from RINEX header. Used for antenna phase centre corrections in precise positioning.
Image file identifier (CAM.Img). One CAM record per shutter trigger, matching each physical image on the SD card.
source_fields.notes: Replaces former L1F_MRK_001 from MRK file.

source_fields.meaning: Precise UTC timestamp of the shutter trigger event derived from CAM.GPSWeek + CAM.GPSTime.
GPS week number at time of capture (CAM.GPSWeek).
Seconds elapsed since the start of the GPS week at shutter trigger (CAM.GPSTime).
Total number of CAM records in the BIN. Equivalent to mrk_image_count. Should match total_images from the SD card.
Boolean: CAM messages were found in the BIN. Equivalent to mrk_file_present. Absence forces fallback to EXIF timestamps for epoch matching.
source_fields.notes: Replaces former L1F_MRK_006. Absence flagged as CAM_RECORDS_MISSING (was MRK_FILE_MISSING).

source_fields.meaning: Target flight altitude AGL in metres, read from CMD.Alt for each NAV_WAYPOINT.
Target cruise speed in m/s, read from DO_CHANGE_SPEED commands (CId=178) in CMD.
Total waypoints in the mission, read from CMD.CTot. NAV_WAYPOINT count specifically used for completion ratio.
Planned launch latitude, read from NAV_TAKEOFF CMD.Lat.
Planned launch longitude, read from NAV_TAKEOFF CMD.Lng.
Planned launch elevation, read from NAV_TAKEOFF CMD.Alt. AGL altitude calculations use this as the ground reference.
Mean actual flight altitude AGL throughout the survey, computed from CTUN.Alt during cruise.
Lowest altitude recorded during the survey (CTUN.Alt min). Includes takeoff/landing if not filtered.
Highest altitude recorded (CTUN.Alt max). Large altitude variance indicates terrain-following issues or wind disturbance.
Total airborne time in seconds, computed as ARM event timestamp delta (DISARM minus ARM).
Number of mission waypoints the drone successfully visited, counted from MISE NAV_WAYPOINT execution events.
Battery level at takeoff, read from BAT.RemPct at the ARM event. Low starting battery increases RTB risk.
source_fields.notes: From BIN BAT messages at ARM event.

source_fields.meaning: Battery level at landing, read from BAT.RemPct at the DISARM event. Very low values indicate near-forced landing.
source_fields.notes: From BIN BAT messages at DISARM event.

source_fields.meaning: Boolean: BIN file exists and parsed successfully. Always TRUE in this consolidated pipeline as the BIN is the primary FC source.
source_fields.notes: No longer aspirational — BIN is the primary FC source.

source_fields.meaning: Duration of the recorded BIN log. Should match flight_duration_sec.
Mean vibration on X axis during cruise (VIBE.VibeX mean). High vibration causes image blur and degrades photogrammetric reconstruction.
source_fields.notes: No longer aspirational — from BIN VIBE messages.

source_fields.meaning: Mean vibration on Y axis during cruise (VIBE.VibeY mean).
Mean vibration on Z axis (VIBE.VibeZ mean). Vertical vibration directly affects image sharpness.
Peak vibration on X axis (VIBE.VibeX max). Spikes indicate turbulence or mechanical issues.
Peak vibration on Y axis (VIBE.VibeY max).
Peak vibration on Z axis (VIBE.VibeZ max).
Variance of drone roll angle during cruise (ATT.Roll). High variance means unstable platform during image capture.
source_fields.notes: No longer aspirational — from BIN ATT messages.

source_fields.meaning: Variance of drone pitch during cruise (ATT.Pitch). Forward/backward tilting affects image footprint.
Variance of drone heading during cruise (ATT.Yaw). Heading instability causes image rows to misalign.
source_fields.notes: No longer aspirational. Naturally large during survey heading changes — interpret over cruise lines only.

source_fields.meaning: Mean gimbal roll during cruise (CAM.R mean). Should be near zero for nadir-looking survey cameras.
source_fields.notes: No longer aspirational — from BIN CAM messages.

source_fields.meaning: Mean gimbal pitch (CAM.P mean). For mapping surveys typically near −90° (nadir); deviations indicate gimbal misconfiguration.
Number of gimbal stabilisation failure events logged. Scanned from ERR/EV messages in the BIN.
source_fields.notes: No longer aspirational. May require event-pattern matching in EV/ERR streams.

source_fields.meaning: Sampling frequency of ATT records in Hz. Higher rate gives more precise variance characterisation.

Target forward (along-track) overlap percentage between consecutive images. Pure operator intent entered once per mission in the JSON form. Used as the planned denominator for overlap_fwd_execution_ratio.
source_fields.notes: Only available from operator — not encoded in BIN waypoint geometry without circular dependency on camera footprint.

source_fields.meaning: Target lateral (cross-track) overlap percentage between adjacent flight lines. Pure operator intent entered once per mission in the JSON form. Used as the planned denominator for overlap_lat_execution_ratio.
source_fields.notes: Only available from operator — see L1F_UI_001 note.

source_fields.meaning: Mean wind speed at 10m height during the flight window, fetched from Open-Meteo historical API using takeoff lat/lng (BIN) and flight_start/end_utc (derived from BIN). Feeds wind_impact_ratio and wind_condition_score.
source_fields.notes: Falls back to ATT-derived proxy if API unavailable — see BIN-internal fallback rule in wind_condition_score script hint.

derived_fields.formula_notes: Fraction of captured images that are usable.
derived_fields.meaning: Fraction of captured images that are usable. Corrupted or unreadable images reduce effective coverage.

derived_fields.formula_notes: Fraction of images with any GPS coordinate in EXIF.
derived_fields.meaning: Fraction of images with any GPS coordinate in EXIF. Images without geotags cannot be used in photogrammetric reconstruction.

derived_fields.formula_notes: Boolean: images in raw uncompressed format.
derived_fields.meaning: Boolean: images are in raw uncompressed format. DNG/RAW retains full radiometric depth.

derived_fields.formula_expression: computed from EXIF GPS positions + image footprint geometry: footprint_width = (sensor_width_mm/focal_length_mm) * actual_altitude; overlap_fwd = 1 - (along_track_distance / footprint_height)
derived_fields.formula_notes: Forward overlap computed from actual EXIF positions, not from any flight log.
derived_fields.meaning: Forward overlap computed from actual image EXIF positions and footprint geometry. Independent of any GCS-computed metric.

derived_fields.formula_expression: same method as image_computed_overlap_fwd_pct, lateral (cross-track) direction
derived_fields.formula_notes: Lateral overlap computed from actual EXIF positions.
derived_fields.meaning: Lateral overlap computed from actual EXIF positions. Captures cases where flight lines drifted apart.

derived_fields.formula_notes: Boolean: camera manufacturer match.
derived_fields.meaning: Boolean: the camera manufacturer in EXIF matches the calibration file. Mismatches mean the calibration applies to different hardware.

derived_fields.formula_notes: Boolean: camera model match.
derived_fields.meaning: Boolean: the camera model in EXIF matches the calibration file.

derived_fields.formula_notes: survey_date taken from BIN CAM gnss_timestamp_utc (first CAM record).
derived_fields.meaning: Age of the camera calibration in months at time of survey. Calibration parameters drift with thermal cycling, vibration, lens handling.

derived_fields.formula_notes: Coefficient of variation — lower = more consistent exposure.
derived_fields.meaning: How stable camera exposure was throughout the flight session. High variance means radiometric inconsistency.

derived_fields.formula_notes: Ratio > 1.0 is ideal (recording started before takeoff and ended after landing).
derived_fields.meaning: Fraction of the flight that has RINEX coverage. Ratio > 1.0 is ideal. Below 1.0 means part of the flight has no rover observations.

derived_fields.formula_notes: Boolean threshold on dual frequency ratio.
derived_fields.meaning: Boolean: the RINEX file is predominantly dual-frequency. Enables ionospheric correction in PPK.

derived_fields.formula_notes: Direct rename of source boolean for indicator clarity.
derived_fields.meaning: Boolean: any gap in RINEX recording exceeds 60 seconds. A gap this long during a short drone flight effectively breaks PPK.

derived_fields.formula_expression: time from first RINEX epoch to first epoch where sat_count >= 4 AND cn0_mean >= 30
derived_fields.formula_notes: Iterate epochs in RINEX file; record time delta from obs_start_utc to first stable epoch.
derived_fields.meaning: How long the rover took to reach stable satellite tracking after recording began.

derived_fields.formula_expression: mean(PDOP values) computed from satellite geometry across all RINEX epochs within the flight window
derived_fields.formula_notes: PDOP must be computed from RINEX satellite positions (not directly stored). Flight window now derived from BIN ARM/DISARM.
derived_fields.meaning: Mean Position Dilution of Precision during the actual flight window. Low PDOP means good satellite geometry.

derived_fields.formula_notes: Same window as mean_pdop_during_flight.
derived_fields.meaning: Worst-case satellite geometry during the flight. A spike above 6.0 during the flight is much worse than the same value while parked.

derived_fields.formula_expression: convert(ARM_event_timestamp) using GPS.GWk + GPS.GMS at the GPS message closest to ARM
derived_fields.formula_notes: One-time conversion in BIN parser.
derived_fields.meaning: True UTC takeoff timestamp, derived from the BIN ARM event by cross-referencing the nearest GPS week + time-of-week. No external clock required.

derived_fields.formula_expression: convert(DISARM_event_timestamp) using GPS.GWk + GPS.GMS at the GPS message closest to DISARM
derived_fields.formula_notes: Same conversion at DISARM.
derived_fields.meaning: True UTC landing timestamp, derived from the BIN DISARM event.

derived_fields.formula_expression: count(MODE transitions into RTL or LAND while ARM_state is True)
derived_fields.formula_notes: From BIN MODE messages.
derived_fields.meaning: Number of mid-flight mode changes into RTL or LAND, used as a proxy for mission interruptions.

derived_fields.formula_notes: From BIN MODE messages.
derived_fields.meaning: Boolean: did the drone initiate a return-to-base mode during the flight.

derived_fields.formula_notes: Cross-file check between BIN CAM records and Images source.
derived_fields.meaning: Derived boolean: number of CAM shutter records in the BIN matches the number of image files on the SD card. Mismatch indicates a recording error.

derived_fields.formula_notes: Shoelace formula on convex hull or polygon of waypoints.
derived_fields.meaning: Survey area polygon area derived from BIN CMD waypoints. Replaces the former planned_area_m2 source field.

derived_fields.formula_notes: Camera params from calibration, altitude from BIN.
derived_fields.meaning: Target GSD derived from planned altitude and camera geometry. Replaces former source field.

derived_fields.formula_notes: Same formula using actual mean altitude from BIN CTUN.
derived_fields.meaning: Achieved GSD computed from actual mean altitude and camera geometry. Replaces former source field.

derived_fields.formula_notes: Footprint per image computed from altitude + sensor geometry; union polygon over all geotagged images.
derived_fields.meaning: Actual coverage percentage of planned area, computed from the union of geotagged image footprints. Replaces former source field.

derived_fields.formula_notes: Ratio near 1.0 = good altitude control.
derived_fields.meaning: How closely the achieved GSD matched the planned GSD. Ratio near 1.0 = good altitude control.

derived_fields.formula_notes: Uses computed actual overlap (L2D_IMG_004) divided by user-input planned overlap.
derived_fields.meaning: How closely achieved forward overlap matched planned overlap.

derived_fields.formula_notes: Same pattern as fwd.
derived_fields.meaning: How closely achieved lateral overlap matched planned overlap.
How closely mean actual altitude matched planned altitude.
Fraction of planned waypoints successfully visited.
Fraction of the planned survey area that was actually flown.

derived_fields.formula_notes: 12 m/s = typical maximum operational wind speed for survey drones.
derived_fields.meaning: Mean wind speed normalised against the operational limit.

derived_fields.formula_expression: std_dev(CTUN altitude values during cruise lines)
derived_fields.formula_notes: Excludes takeoff and landing phases.
derived_fields.meaning: How much the drone's altitude fluctuated during the survey lines.

indicators.meaning: What fraction of captured images are usable? Corrupted images remove coverage — below 30 triggers the critical drone gate.
indicators.source_chain_text: image_validity_ratio <- valid_images / total_images <- Images source file
indicators.gate_action: Sets image_capture_score = 0 (no recoverable survey data). Raises FLAG: CRITICAL_IMAGE_FAILURE.
indicators.v2_1_q_lock_note: Q-DRONE-2: existing hard gate (image_validity < 30). Confirmed chain-level critical-path.

indicators.meaning: What fraction of valid images have GPS coordinates? Images without geotags cannot be used in photogrammetric reconstruction.
indicators.source_chain_text: image_geotag_ratio <- geotagged_images / total_images <- Images source file

indicators.meaning: Do the actual image positions prove sufficient overlap exists? Computed from real EXIF positions, independent of any GCS log.
indicators.source_chain_text: min(image_computed_overlap_fwd_pct, image_computed_overlap_lat_pct) <- EXIF GPS + camera footprint geometry

indicators.meaning: Are images in a format that preserves full radiometric data? DNG and RAW formats preserve full sensor depth.
indicators.source_chain_text: image_format_is_raw <- image_format field <- Images source file

indicators.meaning: How stable was camera exposure throughout the entire flight session? High exposure variance means radiometric inconsistency.
indicators.source_chain_text: exposure_consistency_ratio <- std_dev(EXIF.ExposureTime) / mean(EXIF.ExposureTime) <- Images source file

indicators.meaning: Did the rover record throughout the entire flight with adequate buffer before and after?
indicators.source_chain_text: rinex_coverage_ratio + pre_buffer_sec + post_buffer_sec <- obs_duration_sec / flight_duration_sec
indicators.gate_action: Sets drone_score = 0 (HARD GATE — Q-DRONE-2 lock v2.1). Raises FLAG: RINEX_CRITICAL_GAP. PPK has no rover observations during flight; output is unrecoverable.
indicators.v2_1_q_lock_note: Q-DRONE-2: promoted to chain-level hard gate. Rover coverage gap during flight makes PPK fundamentally impossible.

indicators.meaning: How strong and clean was the satellite signal during the flight? C/N0 below 30dBHz indicates degraded signal.
indicators.source_chain_text: cn0_mean_dbhz + cycle_slip_count <- Rover RINEX source file

indicators.meaning: Is dual-frequency GNSS data available? Enables ionospheric correction critical for PPK precision.
indicators.source_chain_text: dual_freq_available <- dual_freq_ratio > 0.95 <- Rover RINEX source file

indicators.meaning: Were there any critical recording gaps? A gap over 60 seconds during a short drone flight effectively breaks PPK.
indicators.source_chain_text: critical_gap_present <- any_gap_gt_60s <- Rover RINEX source file
indicators.gate_action: HARD RULE: Sets rover_continuity_score = 0. Raises FLAG: RINEX_CRITICAL_GAP.

indicators.meaning: How quickly did the rover reach stable satellite tracking after recording began?
indicators.source_chain_text: rover_acquisition_time_sec <- time from first RINEX epoch to first stable tracking

indicators.meaning: How good was satellite geometry during the actual flight window? PDOP magnifies all measurement errors.
indicators.source_chain_text: mean_pdop_during_flight + max_pdop_during_flight <- computed from satellite positions within BIN-derived flight window

indicators.meaning: What fraction of the planned survey area was actually flown? An unflown area is unflown data.
indicators.source_chain_text: area_coverage_ratio <- actual_coverage_pct / 100 <- L2D_FC_004 derived from image footprints + BIN waypoints
indicators.gate_action: Sets drone_score = 0 (HARD GATE — Q-DRONE-2 lock v2.1). Raises FLAG: COVERAGE_GAP. Mission area not covered; deliverable is fundamentally incomplete.
indicators.v2_1_q_lock_note: Q-DRONE-2: promoted to chain-level hard gate. Mission coverage gap means survey area is not actually surveyed.

indicators.meaning: How closely did the achieved GSD match what was planned? GSD determines the resolution of the deliverable.
indicators.source_chain_text: gsd_execution_ratio <- actual_gsd_cm / planned_gsd_cm <- BIN altitude + EXIF/calibration camera params

indicators.meaning: How closely did achieved overlap match planned overlap? Insufficient overlap creates reconstruction gaps.
indicators.source_chain_text: min(overlap_fwd_execution_ratio, overlap_lat_execution_ratio) <- computed actual overlap (L2D_IMG) / user-input planned (SRC_UI_01)

indicators.meaning: How consistently did the drone maintain the planned altitude?
indicators.source_chain_text: altitude_execution_ratio <- actual_altitude_mean_m / planned_altitude_m <- BIN CTUN + BIN CMD

indicators.meaning: Were wind conditions within safe operating limits during the survey?
indicators.source_chain_text: wind_impact_ratio <- mean_wind_speed_ms / 12.0 <- Open-Meteo API (with BIN ATT fallback)
indicators.v2_1_q_lock_note: Q-DRONE-4: introduces INDICATOR-LEVEL null pattern. When Open-Meteo API unavailable, indicator returns null + WIND_API_FALLBACK flag. Does NOT score 100 (don't make up data) or 0 (not a failure — just unavailable). Composite scoring at block level treats indicator null as "not measured."

indicators.meaning: How much did altitude fluctuate during the survey cruise lines?
indicators.source_chain_text: altitude_variance_m <- std_dev(CTUN altitude) during cruise lines <- BIN CTUN

indicators.meaning: Did the drone complete all planned waypoints?
indicators.source_chain_text: mission_completion_ratio <- waypoints_completed / planned_waypoint_count <- BIN MISE + BIN CMD
indicators.gate_action: vibration_score and gimbal_score from FDR are now ACTIVE (BIN supplies them — no longer disabled).

indicators.meaning: Where did the calibration parameters originate? Physically measured calibration is ground truth.
indicators.source_chain_text: calibration_source <- Camera Calibration source file
indicators.gate_action: Shares logical name with L3I_IMG_007 but uses DIFFERENT thresholds (raises additional flags).

indicators.meaning: Does the calibration file match the actual camera hardware used in this survey?
indicators.source_chain_text: camera_make_match + camera_model_match <- EXIF.Make/Model vs calibration file
indicators.gate_action: Shares logical name with L3I_IMG_006 but uses DIFFERENT thresholds. See sheet 05.

indicators.meaning: How recently was this calibration performed or verified?
indicators.source_chain_text: calibration_age_months <- months_between(calibration_date, survey_date) <- L1F_CAL_007 + L1F_BIN_CAM_002

thresholds.condition_expression: image_format is mixed DNG and JPG

thresholds.condition_text: CV <0.20 (moderate variation — some colour matching needed)
mean <6.0 OR max <8.0 (poor geometry — significant obstruction)

thresholds.condition_expression: API_UNAVAILABLE AND ATT_proxy == calm (combined roll+pitch stdev < 2 deg)
API_UNAVAILABLE AND ATT_proxy == unknown (>5 deg or insufficient data)

building_blocks.purpose: Measures the quality of what the camera subsystem produced. Valid, geotagged images with sufficient overlap are foundational.
building_blocks.gate_action: Sets image_capture_score = 0. Raises FLAG: CRITICAL_IMAGE_FAILURE. No recoverable survey data.
building_blocks.weight_redistribution_rule: Active in all workflows (PPK, RTK, No-Correction). Never disabled.

building_blocks.purpose: Measures the quality of the raw GNSS observations recorded during the flight. The raw material for PPK post-processing.
building_blocks.weight_redistribution_rule: PRIMARY in PPK workflow. Disabled in no-correction workflow; in that case its weight is redistributed across remaining blocks.

building_blocks.purpose: Measures how faithfully the drone executed the planned mission. GSD, overlap, altitude fidelity, coverage, wind, completion.
building_blocks.weight_redistribution_rule: Active in all workflows. vibration_score and gimbal_score from BIN FDR data are now ACTIVE (no longer disabled).

building_blocks.purpose: Answers: 'How reliable is this calibration file for ODM lens correction?' Combines calibration provenance, hardware match, and recency.
building_blocks.weight_redistribution_rule: PARALLEL DELIVERABLE — NOT part of drone_score. CAL_CONF travels with the camera calibration file from the drone subsystem to the Processing Universe.

drone_score.purpose: Answers: 'How good was the drone's contribution to this survey?' Combines image capture, rover GNSS, and mission execution into a single 0–100 score.
drone_score.global_gate_action:
Any one of the following forces drone_score = 0 (HARD GATE — Q-DRONE-2 lock):
  (1) L3I_IMG_001 image_validity_score == 0 → CRITICAL_IMAGE_FAILURE (no usable images)
  (2) L3I_GNSS_001 rover_coverage_score == 0 → RINEX_CRITICAL_GAP (no rover observations during flight)
  (3) L3I_FC_001 mission_coverage_score == 0 → COVERAGE_GAP (mission area not covered)
When any gate fires, overall score is forced to 0. Audit trail must identify which gate(s) fired.
drone_score.severity_of_finding_vocabulary: Indicator-band level severity. Four values, in increasing severity: NONE (band passed) → MINOR (hygiene signal, audit-only, doesn't drive recommendation) → MATERIAL (warrants review) → CRITICAL (warrants resurvey).
drone_score.verification_status_field: Separate categorical field reporting drone-level verification quality. VALUES: DRONE_VERIFIED_HIGH (all blocks passed cleanly); DRONE_VERIFIED_MARGINAL (one or more material findings; review_recommended); DRONE_VERIFIED_FAIL (critical finding present; resurvey_recommended); DRONE_HARD_GATE_FIRED (one of three hard gates fired). NEVER score-gating. Score reflects measurement quality; status reports verification basis.
drone_score.tier_interpretation:
BASE TIER (from numerical score): Gold 90-100; Silver 75-89; Bronze 60-74; Marginal 40-59; Poor <40. 

HARD GATE: When any of three hard gates fires, drone_score = 0. Tier = Poor (HARD GATE). 

Q-DRONE-1/2/3/4/5 lock decisions (v2.1):
  Q-DRONE-1: Deduplicated calibration indicators (removed from BB_IMG_CAPTURE; retained in BB_CAL_CONF).
  Q-DRONE-2: 3 chain-level hard gates (image validity + rover coverage + mission coverage).
  Q-DRONE-3: BB_CAL_CONF retained as 4th block.
  Q-DRONE-4: Wind API failure → indicator-level null (new pattern concept).
  Q-DRONE-5: SELF_CALIBRATED_LENS stays MEDIUM/material.
drone_score.null_handling: drone_score = null when (a) primary source files not provided (raw images, rover RINEX, flight controller log), or (b) all three hard gates would fire (effectively empty survey). Indicator-level null (Q-DRONE-4 new pattern): L3I_FC_005 wind_condition returns null when Open-Meteo API unavailable. Block aggregation treats indicator-null as "not measured" — renormalizes block weight across measured indicators.
drone_score.per_indicator_null_handling: Q-DRONE-4 lock v2.1: Drone introduces INDICATOR-LEVEL null (distinct from GCP's chain-level null). When a specific indicator cannot be measured (e.g., wind API unavailable), that indicator returns null rather than fabricating a score. Block aggregation renormalizes across measured indicators only. Distinguishes "we don't know" from "score=0" and from "score=100." Composite scoring at chain and OJS level must respect indicator-null vs zero.
drone_score.scope_note: Drone is one of three capture chains (drone, base_station, GCP). drone_score feeds upward into capture_score (eventual OJS integration layer). 4 blocks: Image Capture (0.40) + Rover GNSS (0.30) + Mission Execution (0.20) + Calibration Confidence (0.10). PPK workflow — depends on rover RINEX + base RINEX downstream.
drone_score.source_file_set: Pilot delivers: (1) raw images (SRC_IMG_01), (2) Rover RINEX (SRC_GNSS_01), (3) ArduPilot .BIN (SRC_FC_BIN), (4) JSON metadata form with 2 fields (SRC_UI_01). Pipeline auto-fetches Open-Meteo API (SRC_API_01). Camera calibration (SRC_IMG_02) resolved automatically by ODM.

flags.meaning: No recoverable survey data — too many corrupted/unreadable images. Causes image_capture_score = 0 and drone_score = 0.
Actual image positions do not provide enough overlap for photogrammetric reconstruction.

flags.condition: Images are a mix of DNG and JPG formats
flags.meaning: Inconsistent radiometric quality across the dataset.
Lighting changed significantly during the survey — radiometric calibration affected.
A RINEX recording gap of 60s+ effectively breaks the carrier phase solution. Hard rule zeros rover_continuity_score.
Rover took over 5 minutes to reach stable tracking — limited pre-flight convergence time.
Satellite geometry was poor during the flight — significant sky obstruction.
Planned survey area not fully flown. Unflown area cannot be recovered without re-flight.
Survey flown in wind exceeding safe operating limits — image blur and unstable flight.

flags.condition: Open-Meteo API unavailable; ATT-derived proxy band == unknown
flags.meaning: Wind speed could not be confirmed from API and BIN attitude proxy was inconclusive — wind_condition_score derived from fallback band only.
flags.v2_1_q_lock: Q-DRONE-4: triggers indicator-level null on L3I_FC_005 (wind_condition_score). Different from missing input — explicit "infrastructure couldn't verify" condition. Composite scoring should respect indicator-null.

flags.meaning: Significant altitude fluctuation during cruise — GSD inconsistent across the site.
ODM estimated lens parameters from the images themselves — circular dependency with the reconstruction.
flags.v2_1_q_lock: Q-DRONE-5: stays MEDIUM (material). Self-calibration works for most cases; flagging at review level lets operator decide whether to insist on pre-calibration based on accuracy requirements.

flags.meaning: No calibration file present at all.
Calibration file is for a different camera than the one used in this survey.
Calibration older than 36 months — lens parameters may have drifted.
Calibration date cannot be determined — recency cannot be assessed.
Drone produced no recoverable data. drone_score=0. All downstream processing stops.
No CAM records found in the BIN. PPK falls back to less-precise EXIF timestamps for epoch matching. (Was MRK_FILE_MISSING.)
Number of CAM shutter records in the BIN does not match total_images on the SD card. Indicates a recording error or missing images.

script_hints.pseudocode:
ratio = valid_images / total_images
if ratio >= 0.99: return 100
elif ratio >= 0.97: return 88
elif ratio >= 0.94: return 72
elif ratio >= 0.90: return 55
else:
    raise_flag("CRITICAL_IMAGE_FAILURE")
    return 20

ratio = geotagged_images / total_images
if ratio >= 0.99: return 100
elif ratio >= 0.97: return 88
elif ratio >= 0.93: return 72
elif ratio >= 0.88: return 55
else: return 20

m = min(image_computed_overlap_fwd_pct, image_computed_overlap_lat_pct)
if m >= 70: return 100
elif m >= 60: return 88
elif m >= 50: return 72
elif m >= 40: return 50
else:
    raise_flag("INSUFFICIENT_OVERLAP")
    return 20

if image_format in ('DNG','RAW'): return 100
elif image_format == 'JPG': return 75
else:
    raise_flag('MIXED_IMAGE_FORMAT')
    return 55

cv = std_dev(EXIF.ExposureTime_list) / mean(EXIF.ExposureTime_list)
if cv < 0.05: return 100
elif cv < 0.10: return 88
elif cv < 0.20: return 72
elif cv < 0.35: return 50
else:
    raise_flag("HIGH_EXPOSURE_VARIATION")
    return 25

if not calibration_file_present: return 40
if camera_make_match and camera_model_match: return 100
elif camera_make_match: return 60
else: return 20

if calibration_source == 'CB_LIBRARY': return 100
elif calibration_source == 'ODM_DATABASE': return 80
elif calibration_source == 'SELF_CALIBRATED': return 55
else: return 30

r = rinex_coverage_ratio
if r >= 1.10 and pre_buffer_sec >= 120 and post_buffer_sec >= 60: return 100
elif r >= 1.00 and pre_buffer_sec >= 60: return 88
elif r >= 0.95: return 72
elif r >= 0.85: return 50
else: return 20

if cn0_mean_dbhz >= 35 and cycle_slip_count < 5: return 100
elif cn0_mean_dbhz >= 32 and cycle_slip_count < 10: return 85
elif cn0_mean_dbhz >= 28 or cycle_slip_count < 20: return 65
else: return 35
return 100 if dual_freq_available else 55

# HARD RULE
if critical_gap_present:
    raise_flag("RINEX_CRITICAL_GAP")
    return 0
return 100

t = rover_acquisition_time_sec
if t < 60: return 100
elif t < 120: return 88
elif t < 300: return 65
else:
    raise_flag("SLOW_ROVER_ACQUISITION")
    return 30

m, x = mean_pdop_during_flight, max_pdop_during_flight
if m < 1.5 and x < 2.5: return 100
elif m < 2.0 and x < 3.5: return 88
elif m < 3.0 and x < 5.0: return 65
elif m < 6.0 or x < 8.0: return 35
else:
    raise_flag("POOR_SKY_VIEW_DURING_FLIGHT")
    return 10
# Flight window now derived from BIN ARM/DISARM events (no GCS telemetry needed).

r = area_coverage_ratio   # actual_coverage_pct (derived L2D_FC_004) / 100
if r >= 0.99: return 100
elif r >= 0.97: return 88
elif r >= 0.93: return 72
elif r >= 0.88: return 50
else:
    raise_flag("COVERAGE_GAP")
    return 20

r = gsd_execution_ratio   # actual_gsd_cm (L2D_FC_003) / planned_gsd_cm (L2D_FC_002)
if 0.92 <= r <= 1.05: return 100
elif 0.85 <= r <= 1.10: return 85
elif 0.78 <= r <= 1.18: return 68
else: return 40

r_fwd = image_computed_overlap_fwd_pct / planned_overlap_fwd_pct   # planned from SRC_UI_01
r_lat = image_computed_overlap_lat_pct / planned_overlap_lat_pct
r = min(r_fwd, r_lat)
if 0.95 <= r <= 1.10: return 100
elif 0.88 <= r <= 1.15: return 85
elif 0.80 <= r <= 1.20: return 68
else: return 35

r = actual_altitude_mean_m / planned_altitude_m   # both from BIN
if 0.95 <= r <= 1.05: return 100
elif 0.88 <= r <= 1.10: return 85
elif 0.80 <= r <= 1.18: return 65
else: return 35

# Primary: Open-Meteo API
try:
    w = fetch_open_meteo(takeoff_lat, takeoff_lng, flight_start_utc, flight_end_utc).mean_wind_10m
    if w < 5: return 100
    elif w < 8: return 85
    elif w < 10: return 65
    elif w < 12: return 45
    else:
        raise_flag("HIGH_WIND_SURVEY")
        return 20
except ApiUnavailable:
    # Fallback: BIN ATT proxy
    combined_stdev = sqrt(roll_variance_deg + pitch_variance_deg)
    if combined_stdev < 2.0: return 85    # likely calm
    elif combined_stdev < 5.0: return 60  # moderate
    else:
        raise_flag("WIND_API_FALLBACK")
        return 30

v = altitude_variance_m   # std_dev(CTUN.Alt during cruise)
if v < 2: return 100
elif v < 5: return 88
elif v < 10: return 65
elif v < 20: return 40
else:
    raise_flag("HIGH_ALTITUDE_VARIANCE")
    return 20

r = waypoints_completed / planned_waypoint_count   # both from BIN
if r == 1.00: return 100
elif r >= 0.97: return 85
elif r >= 0.93: return 65
else: return 30

# CAL_CONF version raises additional flags vs L3I_IMG_007
if calibration_source == 'CB_LIBRARY': return 100
elif calibration_source == 'ODM_DATABASE': return 80
elif calibration_source == 'SELF_CALIBRATED':
    raise_flag('SELF_CALIBRATED_LENS')
    return 55
else:
    raise_flag('NO_CALIBRATION_FILE')
    return 30

# CAL_CONF version: 3 bands, harder penalty for mismatch
if camera_make_match and camera_model_match: return 100
elif camera_make_match: return 65
else:
    raise_flag("CALIBRATION_CAMERA_MISMATCH")
    return 15

if calibration_date is None:
    raise_flag('CALIBRATION_DATE_UNKNOWN')
    return 50
m = months_between(calibration_date, survey_date)   # survey_date from BIN first CAM record
if m < 6: return 100
elif m < 12: return 88
elif m < 24: return 72
elif m < 36: return 55
else:
    raise_flag('CALIBRATION_POTENTIALLY_OUTDATED')
    return 30

# drone_score (PPK workflow) — post BIN-consolidation
# 1. Parse the 3 pilot files: images (SD card), Rover RINEX, ArduPilot .BIN
# 2. Load the 2-field JSON metadata form (SRC_UI_01)
# 3. Derive BIN-level facts: CAM records, CMD waypoints, ARM/DISARM UTC, CTUN altitudes, BAT, MODE, VIBE, ATT, MISE
# 4. Compute all L2D_* derived fields
# 5. Fetch wind from Open-Meteo API using BIN-derived takeoff lat/lng + flight_start/end_utc
#    (fall back to ATT-proxy if API unavailable)
# 6. Compute all indicators (sheet 04) using thresholds (sheet 05)
# 7. Apply hard-rule gates: L3I_GNSS_004 (critical gap) and L3I_IMG_001 (validity < 30)
# 8. Compute building blocks (sheet 06) using composition weights (sheet 07)
# 9. If image_capture_score == 0 -> drone_score = 0, raise DRONE_CRITICAL_FAILURE, stop
# 10. drone_score = 0.40*image_capture_score + 0.35*rover_gnss_score + 0.25*mission_execution_score
# 11. CAL_CONF computed in parallel (sheet 06 BB_CAL_CONF). Travels with calibration file to Processing Universe.
